Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions - duplicate entry.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Bracco, Esq. of Philip M. Andersen & Associates, Tel. 925.225.6838, by Court Call; No other appearance.

RULING: Defendant's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter was discussed. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS MARIA GOMEZ, ROCIO GOMEZ, JOEL GOMEZ's Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions, as follows: "[A] trial court may order a terminating sanction for discovery abuse 'after considering the totality of the circumstances: [the] conduct of the party to determine if the actions were willful; the detriment to the propounding party; and the number of formal and informal attempts to obtain the discovery.' [Citation.] Generally, '[a] decision to order terminating sanctions should not be made lightly." Los Defensores, Inc. v. Gomez (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 377, 390. The Court finds that Plaintiff Levella Johnson has failed to comply with this Court's orders of September 22, 2014 requiring her to 1) serve written verified answers and responses (without objection other than privilege) to moving parties' Special Interrogatories, Limited Form Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents; and 2) to appear for deposition prior to October 20. Pursuant to CCP 2030.300(e) and 2031.300(c), the Court GRANTS the motion in part as follows: Within 10 days of the date of service of this order on Plaintiff, Plaintiff Levella Johnson shall serve written verified answers and responses (without objections) to moving parties' Special and Limited Form Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents; and 2) shall appear for deposition prior to February 27, 2015. The Court advises Plaintiff Levella Johnson that if she fails to comply with this Order (i.e. does not provide Defendants' counsel with written verified answers and responses to the Special and Limited Form Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents and does not appear for deposition), her lawsuit against Defendants Rocio, Joel and Maria Gomez may be dismissed due to her repeated failure to participate in discovery. Defendants' motion for a terminating sanction is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE and may be renewed if Plaintiff fails to comply with this order. Defendants' request for $559.48 in monetary sanctions is DENIED for failure to state in the motion the basis for the calculation of the requested amount of sanctions. See CCP 2023.040 (requiring declaration setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought). ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Sanford Kingsley; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
